name: ctpV
function: P-type ATPase pump involved in metal efflux (PMID 40196583).  Also described as a putative cation transporter (PMID 10500215) or copper exporter (PMID 21925112).
drug_susc_impact: Mutants lacking CtpV are more susceptible to copper (PMID 22361385). Increased susceptibility to copper-containing drugs (PMID 18263720). Involved in conferring cadmium tolerance, potentially affecting drug susceptibility (PMID 40196583). Potentially interesting targets for discovering treatments against mycobacteria (PMID 21925112).
infection_impact: Part of a multipronged system to balance intracellular copper levels (PMID 22361385).  Causes zinc retention within the mycobacterial cytoplasm, leading to impaired intracellular growth (PMID 21925112). Contributes to intracellular survival during infection (PMID 21925112).  Mutants display altered virulence patterns in vivo (PMID 21925112). Generates a robust Th1-biased T-cell response in mice (PMID 39663556). Essential for M. tuberculosis survival during infection by conferring multi-metal resistance (PMID 40196583). Potential role in maintaining M. tuberculosis survival during infection (PMID 18263720).
